Database Systems.question: For a database designed using relational database, one of the following arguments is not correct.
options: Duplicate tuples are not allowed in a relation
Every relation must have a unique identifier
A table can have more than one primary key
Foreign key can take a value of NULL
Database Systems.answer: A table can have more than one primary key

Database Systems.question: Which of the following contains information about the structure of a database?
An ________ is a set of objects of the same class that share the same properties.
DBMS has three categories of languages and one of this is DML. DML is used for_________________
options: describe the logical setup or structure of the databse
specify the storage structure of the database system
processing the content of the database
changing the structure of the database
Database Systems.answer: processing the content of the database

Database Systems.question: In relational database system, a conceptual schema is
options: specification of how user data is actually stored
description of how users perceive the data
a systematic way of representing data elements

Database Systems.question: DBMS systems can be made to execute transactions. Transaction processing is a process which does NOT include__________
options: initiating a response or verifying an action
Database Systems.answer: initiating a response or verifying an action

Database Systems.question: In database systems terminology, "Data Independence" is more described as
options: programs are not dependent on the physical attributes of data.
data in not related with other data
programs are dependent on the logical attributes of data.
data is defined separately and not included in programs.
Database Systems.answer: programs are not dependent on the physical attributes of data.

Database Systems.question: Among the relational algebra operators, _______, is used to produce a result which contains attributes of two or more relations.
One of the following security mechanisms can NOT be enforced without using database management system software:
Which name must be unique within a database ?
Assuming that there is an Employee table with attributes ID, NAME, SALARY and AGE, where all are mandatory attributes, which of the following is a valid SQL statement?
options: SELECT NAME FROM EMPLOYEE WHERE SALARY = NULL
SELECT AGE FROM EMPLOYEE WHERE EMPLOYEE=AGE

Database Systems.question: When a table from a database is stored at multiple servers and locations, it is known as:
Appropriate controls must be designed to ensure that no field takes on a value that is outside of the range of legal values. This rule is referred as____________.
A relation in 1NF and that contains no multivalued attributes, but has non-primary key attributes dependent on part of the primary key is in its:
If a set of attributes, K, in relation table R is a foreign key on the table R, then:_____
options: K cannot assume a value of NULL in R
Every non-null value of K, appearing in relation R, must also occur as a value for K in S.
There is another relation schema (say S) with the attributes K where K is a foreign key for S
Database Systems.answer: Every non-null value of K, appearing in relation R, must also occur as a value for K in S.

Which of the following is true about the name of a variable/identity?
options: Keywords/reserved words can be used as variable names
A variable name can start with a number
An underscore can be a variable name
Using descriptive names for variable names doesn’t add any value to the clarity and
Computer Programming.answer: An underscore can be a variable name

Computer Programming.question: If a function has this parameter (int *a), all of the following will be correct except:
options: A change made through “*a” affects the variable in the calling function.
The argument in a call to this function can just be the name of an ordinary
Dereferencing ‘a’ is necessary to change the value of the memory that the variable
The function cannot be called with a constant value as an argument.
Computer Programming.answer: The argument in a call to this function can just be the name of an ordinary variable.

Computer Programming.question: Which one is the correct output of the following program? * #include <iostream> using namespace std; int main() { float mark=70.0; char grade_letter; if(mark>=90 && mark<=100) { grade_letter='A'; cout<<grade_letter; } else if(mark>=70 && mark<90) { grade_letter='B'; cout<<grade_letter; } else if(mark>=50 && mark<=70) { grade_letter='C'; cout<<grade_letter; } else { grade_letter='F'; 1/1 cout<<grade_letter; } return 0; }
The result of the following program is_____. * #include <iostream> using namespace std; int main() { for(int i=0;i<5;i++) { for(int j=i;j<5-i;j++) { cout<<j+i; } cout<<endl; } return 0;

Assuming there is a statement myfile.close(); immediately after the write to the file above, what is the purpose of the line of code myfile.close()?
options: It detaches the file pointer object myfile from the sample.txt file
It releases any resources being used by the program
It updates the file size of sample.txt accordingly

Data Communication and Computer Networks.question: Which of the following is not a component of a computer network? *
Which of the following is a form of signal that is transmitted through optical fiber?
The transport protocol that is used to create end-to-end communication with acknowledgement is
Which of the following network device works at physical layer?
The command used to know the IP address of servers over the internet ping
Which of the following does not use tcp as transport protocol? *
Which of the following is not a media access control method?
Which access method is used by the GSM network?
Pick the true statement
options: Ethernet is a protocol that works at the data link layer
DHCP helps to map domain names with IP
Manchester encoding techniques work to encode data from application layer to transport layer
SSH is used to compress data to transport from one network to another
Data Communication and Computer Networks.answer: Ethernet is a protocol that works at the data link layer

Data Communication and Computer Networks.question: Which of the following is not true about IP address?
options: Class E IP addresses can be assigned as IP addresses for computers in networks
The total number of IP addresses in Class A is greater than that of class B
88.88.88.88 is a class A IP address
One can know Class B addresses if the numbers in the first octet are between 128 and 191
Data Communication and Computer Networks.answer: Class E IP addresses can be assigned as IP addresses for computers in networks

Data Communication and Computer Networks.question: Which of the following IP address belongs to class D?
An ARP query packet is encapsulated in *
options: A data link layer frame addressed to a specific adapter (NIC)
A data link layer broadcast frame
A data link layer multicast frame
Data Communication and Computer Networks.answer: A data link layer broadcast frame

Data Communication and Computer Networks.question: Which of the following protocol information is not found in the IP Packet?
A NIC of a computer has been assigned an IP address of 130.31.128.60 with a mask of 255.255.255.224. To which subnet does the IP address belong?

Information Systems Project Management.question: The five project management process groups are:
options: initiating, planning, executing, monitoring & controlling, and closing.
planning, checking, directing, monitoring, and recording.
initiating, executing, monitoring, evaluating, and closing.
planning, executing, directing, closing, and delivering.
Information Systems Project Management.answer: initiating, planning, executing, monitoring & controlling, and closing.

Information Systems Project Management.question: A project can be defined as:
options: a set of sequential activities performed in a process or system.
a revenue generating activity that needs to be accomplished while achieving customer satisfaction.
a temporary endeavour undertaken to create a unique product, service or result.
an ongoing endeavour undertaken to meet customer or market requirements.
Information Systems Project Management.answer: a temporary endeavour undertaken to create a unique product, service or result.

Information Systems Project Management.question: A project manager for a software project is well regarded by people in her company for her programming skills. She has a good reputation among the senior managers as well as the employees under her supervision. Which best describes the power the project manager is exerting?
Much of a project manager’s time is spent: *
In sequencing activities using the precedence diagramming method which of the logical relationships is most frequently used?
In understanding the project environment, all of the following are true except:
options: projects are planned or implemented in a social, economic, and environmental context.
projects may have intended positive and/or negative impacts.
projects may have unintended positive and/or negative impacts.
the project team should rarely consider the political and physical environmental contexts of the project.
Information Systems Project Management.answer: the project team should rarely consider the political and physical environmental contexts of the project.

Information Systems Project Management.question: The project manager has the highest level of independence and authority in a ____________ organization.
A project manager has just become the manager of a project. The document that recognizes the existence of the project is called:
What is the main goal of project cost management?
options: to complete a project for as little cost as possible
to complete a project within an approved budget
to provide truthful and accurate cost information on projects
to distribute the project budget equally throughout the project
Information Systems Project Management.answer: to complete a project within an approved budget

Information Systems Project Management.question: Strategies typically used to deal with threats or risks that may have negative impacts on project objectives if they occur include all of the following EXCEPT:
A project cost that can never be fully retrieved (returned) is:
Who has overall responsibility for quality planning?
A matrix that maps persons to tasks in the work breakdown structure is called

You are the project manager for a technical implementation project. The customer has requested that you include the after-the-project costs, such as maintenance and service. This is an example of which one of the following.
Which of the following is most true about quality?
options: It will cost more money to build quality into the project.
It will cost less money to build quality into the project process.
Information Systems Project Management.answer: It will cost less money to build quality into the project process.

Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.question: The term Artificial Intelligence was coined by ____________ in ______________.
Which one of the following describes Artificial Intelligent?
options: It is a science that enables us to create a machine that does everything.
It is about programming a machine with your own Intelligence.
It aims at creating machines that perform tasks that require intelligence.
Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.answer: It aims at creating machines that perform tasks that require intelligence.

Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.question: The following are the key characteristics of an intelligent system except
options: Learn from experience and adapt to changing environment.
They know the outcome of their action.
Tolerate error and ambiguity in communication.
Interact with human using natural language and speech.
Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.answer: They know the outcome of their action.

Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.question: Suppose you are asked to design an agent that acts on the basis of condition-action rules and internal state, besides the current percept. What type of agent do you design?
In which one of the following problem types the agent has no knowledge of what its actions do and about the environment?
One of the following is true about constraint satisfaction problem. *
options: A state is represented by variables and values for each variables.
A solution is a sequence of actions.
A state is considered as atomic indivisible.
A solution is a complete assignment.
Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.answer: A state is represented by variables and values for each variables.

Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.question: Suppose you are asked to implement a search algorithm for a particular problem. Your boss told you that your search algorithm need to return only the final or the goal state. Which class of search algorithm will you use?
One of the following is not true about Hill-Climbing search algorithm. *
options: It is a local search algorithm.
It considers a state space as a graph.
It stops searching when no neighbour has higher value than the current state.
Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.answer: It considers a state space as a graph.

Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence.question: Which data structure is best to implement Depth First Search?
Which of the following is not the property or requirement of knowledge representation languages?
Which one of the following is a knowledge representation language?
Which one of the following First Order Logic sentence represents the statement "All cats like milk"?
The component/s of an Expert system is/are_________.
______________ learning algorithm uses explicit input-output feedback.
A decision tree learning algorithm:
options: selects an attribute with the lowest disorder or entropy for spliting the data.
selects an attribute with the highest information gain for spliting the data.

Systems Analysis and Design.question: In Relational Data Modeling, which of the following is false
options: The goal of normalization is to produce well-structured relations while the goal of denormalization is more of performance
“Entries in cells are simple” in relational data model means that at the intersection of a column and a row there should be a single value
Any relation with single attribute forming the primary key is already in third normal form.
Eliminating partial dependency is the target of second normal form.
Systems Analysis and Design.answer: Any relation with single attribute forming the primary key is already in third normal form.

Systems Analysis and Design.question: Referring to the following ER-Diagram, which of the following statements are false?
options: All tuples of the lease entity must have corresponding matching records from Client entity.
The " 0...1" multiplicity indicates an optional participation.

Systems Analysis and Design.question: Referring to the ER-diagram in question number 2, which one of the statements is TRUE?
options: A branch registers exactly one client.
A client must hold a lease for a property.
A property for lease can be owned by more than one owner.
A staff may or may not manage a branch.
Systems Analysis and Design.answer: A staff may or may not manage a branch.

Systems Analysis and Design.question: The logic of processes in a DFD can be expressed by of all of the following except?
Given the following picture, which type of installation is implied by it
In software project management, a deliverable is *
options: A defined work with clear beginning and end dates
A Tangible and verifiable product of work
A hierarchical breakdown of project activities
Systems Analysis and Design.answer: A Tangible and verifiable product of work

Systems Analysis and Design.question: Which of the following type of Alpha Testing is about testing the system to see if the system withstands an attempt to break it down?

________ is a collection of artefacts, including your use case model, user interface, domain model, change cases model, and supplementary specifications that describe the requirements for a system. A professor in the university offers courses. At the end of each semester,  he/she is responsible to submit grade to a department and a registrar. The registrar maintains students' grades and computes CGPA for each student. Students receive grades for the courses they took along with CGPA in a slip.
Given the above business description, which of the following could be actors?
options: Student, Registrar, Professor, Course, and Grade.

Systems Analysis and Design.question: Referring to the business description in question number 2, what could be a use case (s)?
Referring to the business description in question number 2, what are the possible classes?
options: Professor, Course, Student, Grade, and Registrar.
Professor, Course, Student, Grade, Registrar and CGPA.
Systems Analysis and Design.answer: Professor, Course, Student, Grade, and Registrar.

Systems Analysis and Design.question: What does Register() refer to in the following sequence diagram? *
options: The lifeline of the student object.

Systems Analysis and Design.question: Referring to the following diagram, can you identify the subclasses?
Refer to the class diagram under question number14 Which of the following are the behavior/methods of classes?
options: Student Number and Average Mark in Student Class.
Purchase Parking Pass in Person Class.
Systems Analysis and Design.answer: Purchase Parking Pass in Person Class.

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: What is Information?
options: Dat organized in a meaningful manner to be used in decision making

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: Which of the following is not true about Boolean IR model?
options: The Model penalizes documents when they contain term that is not in the query
Ranking of retrieved documents is possible
Term weighting is always binary weight
Introduction to Information Retrieval.answer: The Model penalizes documents when they contain term that is not in the query

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: Which methods can be applied to remove stop words from a corpus (text document collection)?
The query operation NOT (T1 AND T2) will retrieve documents that ______________________.
options: Contain T1 but not T2 and T2 but not T1

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: An inverted index arranges data in a sorted order as per _________________.
What is the metric used to measure the rarity of a term in a corpus (text document collection)?
The heuristic process of cutting off one or more letters from the beginning or end of words to extract word classes from inflected words is called __________
Which of the following is true?
options: Recall and precision often increase proportionally
Precision decreases when a non-relevant document is retrieved
Corpus size increases proportionally with vocabulary size
Recall decreases when a non-relevant document is retrieved
Introduction to Information Retrieval.answer: Precision decreases when a non-relevant document is retrieved

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: Which of the following is true about Stemming?
options: It increases the recall and reduces the precision
It often has a negative effect on the retrieval model
Introduction to Information Retrieval.answer: It increases the recall and reduces the precision

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: What is the meaning of "error of commission"?
options: When a relevant document is not retrieved
When a Non-relevant document is predicted to be Relevant
When all documents in the corpus are retreived
Introduction to Information Retrieval.answer: When a Non-relevant document is predicted to be Relevant

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: Recall will be 100% when _________________________.
options: all the relevant documents in the corpus are retrieved
recall is much greater than precision
all the documents in the corpus are relevant
Introduction to Information Retrieval.answer: all the relevant documents in the corpus are retrieved

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: Which of the following is not true?
options: With correct query, data retrieval works 100% accurately.
Information retrieval works 100% accurately if given the correct query.
Data retrieval can achieve better result on unstructured corpus.
Introduction to Information Retrieval.answer: Data retrieval can achieve better result on unstructured corpus.

Introduction to Information Retrieval.question: The harmonic mean of recall and precision will give us __________________.

Suppose the vocabulary size of a corus having 100 terms is 10 and that of a corpus having 500 terms is 25. What is the estimated vocabulary size of a corpus having 10000 terms if we take beta to be 0.5? (Hint: use Heaps Law)

Suppose you have the following five documents in your corpus D1 = {t1, t2, t3, t4, t5}, D2 = {t1, t2, t3, t4}, D3 = {t2, t4, t6, t8}, D4 = {t1, t3, t5, t7}, and D5 = {t2, t3, t4} where t stands for term. Which documents will be retrieved for the query Q=t1 AND (t2 OR (NOT t3)) using Boolean Model.

Which of the following property is used to align elements of a flex-box container to the center on the main axis ?
Which of the following following statement is not correct about the grid and flex-box layout systems?
options: Grid layout system is a two dimensional layout system
Flex-box layout system is a one dimensonal layout system
It is possible to nest flex-box inside grid and vice versa
By using flex-box we can't design a layout that can be done using grid layout system
Internet Programming.answer: By using flex-box we can't design a layout that can be done using grid layout system

How many rows will be there when rendering the following HTML code on a browser?
......
......
......
......
Among the following events, which one originates from a mouse?
Which of the following is not true about server-side scripting language ?
options: It is executed on the server
Has access to the server operating system
It has to be translated to the corresponding client side language before rendered on the browser
It can be executed by the browser
Internet Programming.answer: It can be executed by the browser

Internet Programming.question: Which of the following is the correct syntax to write a PHP code?
Which is the right way of declaring a variable in PHP?
Which is the right way of declaring a variable in PHP?

Information System Security.question: The likelihood of a threat source taking advantage of a vulnerability is called _____________.
Which of the following statements about policy, standards, guidelines, or procedures is correct?
options: Standards are mandatory step-by-step, detailed actions required to complete a task successfully
Procedures are mandatory requirements that support individual policies
Guideline is a high-level statement of enterprise beliefs, goals, and the general means for their attainment

Information System Security.question: In developing a security awareness program, it is MOST important to __________.
options: Identify weaknesses in line management support
Understand the corporate culture and how it will affect security
Understand employees’ preferences for information security
Know what security awareness products are available
Information System Security.answer: Understand the corporate culture and how it will affect security

Information System Security.question: Once the key risks have been assessed, what action is unacceptable for very low risks?
What aspect is considered your weakest link in any security solution?
As an information system security principle, “Integrity” is all about _________.
options: preventing unauthorized subjects from modifying information.
allowing only unauthorized subjects to modify information.
preventing unauthorized subjects from accessing information.
allowing only authorized subjects access to information.
Information System Security.answer: preventing unauthorized subjects from modifying information.

Information System Security.question: Which of the following is most relevant to determining the maximum effective cost of access control?
options: Budget planning related to base versus incremental spending.
The value of information that is protected
The cost to replace lost data
Information System Security.answer: The value of information that is protected

Information System Security.question: When a user logs onto a computer system and is asked for their mother’s maiden name, which of the following aspects is the system ensuring?
Which one of the following is an incorrect explanation in relation to layers in information systems security Defense-in-Depth Strategy?
options: Communications security is to protect an organization’s communications media, technology, and content.
Network security is to protect networking components, connections, and contents.
Personal security is to protect the details of a particular operation or series of activities.
Physical security is to protect the physical items, objects, or areas of an organization from unauthorized access and misuse.
Information System Security.answer: Network security is to protect networking components, connections, and contents.

Information System Security.question: How should a security risk be handled when the cost of the countermeasures outweighs the cost of the risk?
Why must senior management endorse a security policy?
options: So that they will accept ownership for security within the organization.
So that employees will follow the policy directives.
So that external bodies will recognize the organization’s commitment to security.
So that they can be held legally accountable.
Information System Security.answer: So that employees will follow the policy directives.

Information System Security.question: Which of the following statements is not correct in relation to information security culture?
options: Many losses are not caused by users of technology and faulty human behavior but rather by lack of technology or faulty technology.
The behavior of employees and their interaction with computer systems have a significant impact on the security of information.
Although the technical aspect of information security needs due attention, a more serious and under-rated aspect of information security is the human element.
The purpose of information security culture is to address the various human factors that can affect an organization’s overall information security efforts.
Information System Security.answer: Many losses are not caused by users of technology and faulty human behavior but rather by lack of technology or faulty technology.

Information System Security.question: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: Identification of security requirements at an enterprise level is performed during design phase of security system development life cycle.
It is impossible to obtain perfect security even if organizations use maximum security control measures.
Spoofing attack is about the prevention of authorized access to resources
A Denial of service or degrading of service attack is situation in which one person or program successfully imitate another by falsifying data and thereby gaining an illegitimate advantage.
Information System Security.answer: It is impossible to obtain perfect security even if organizations use maximum security control measures.

Information System Security.question: The root cause of some discovered vulnerabilities of an application might reasonably be which of the following?
options: The developers did not invest enough money and effort during the development
The government regulators were smarter than the developers expected
Security vulnerabilities are hidden and therefore acceptable to leave in place
The designers had too much technical training
Information System Security.answer: The developers did not invest enough money and effort during the development

Information System Security.question: The integrity threat can be exemplified by which of the following scenarios?
options: Customer records were hidden, but one might have been slightly garbled
The entire employee database was posted to the Internet
Every customer record is exposed by nation states
Every customer record is stolen by hackers
Information System Security.answer: Customer records were hidden, but one might have been slightly garbled
